div.p: Connect Claude Code, Cursor, Continue.dev, or Gemini CLI to your Odoo
Use your Odoo account
Name it, set permissions
Ready config for your tool
MCP requires a local client app on your computer. Browser-only chat UIs cannot connect directly — they don't have access to your network or local ports.

tr.td: Run terminal command from your config
Native MCP connector — paste URL directly. Requires public URL (ngrok / cloud).
Python bridge — works with localhost too

div.label: MCP Server Name *

input.name: mcp_server_name
input.placeholder: e.g. odoo-acme (no spaces, used in AI client config)
input.title: No spaces allowed

div.small: Short unique slug — shown in your AI client as the server name.

t.p: No keys yet — create your first key above.

div.span: ... Rate: /hr Used: times Last: Never used

form.onsubmit: return confirm('Regenerate this key? The old key will stop working immediately.')
return confirm('Revoke this key? It will stop working immediately.')

ol.li: Go to chatgpt.com → sidebar → Apps
Click New App → Connection → Server URL tab
Paste the URL above. Auth: No Auth. Click Create.

div.p: Replace [YOUR_KEY]. Requires public URL (Odoo.sh / ngrok — not localhost).
Replace [YOUR_KEY] with your key. Lost it? Regenerate →
Add to .cursor/mcp.json. Replace [YOUR_KEY].
Merge into ~/.continue/config.json. Replace [YOUR_KEY] and [SERVER-NAME].
① Empty config — paste full block:
② Already has other servers — add only this inside mcpServers{}, comma after previous entry:
Replace [YOUR_KEY]. No trailing comma on last entry. Requires Node.js (node -v).
Merge into ~/.gemini/settings.json. Install: npm i -g @google/generative-ai-cli

div.pre:
# pip install openai mcp # No native MCP in ChatGPT — use Python bridge: import asyncio from mcp import ClientSession from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client from openai import OpenAI
MCP_URL = "/mcp/v1" MCP_KEY = "[YOUR_KEY]"
async def get_tools(): async with streamablehttp_client(MCP_URL, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {MCP_KEY}"}) as (r,w,_): async with ClientSession(r, w) as s: await s.initialize() return (await s.list_tools()).tools
tools = asyncio.run(get_tools()) oai_tools = [{"type":"function","function":{ "name": t.name, "description": t.description, "parameters": t.inputSchema}} for t in tools]
client = OpenAI() # OPENAI_API_KEY env var response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", tools=oai_tools, messages=[{"role":"user","content":"Your question here"}] )

div.p: Replace [YOUR_KEY] with your key. For ChatGPT: paste this URL directly as Server URL (No Auth). Requires public URL.
Copy or download your configs now — the plain key is shown only once.

div.div: ⚠ Save this key now — it cannot be retrieved again. Use Download Config below to save everything.

div.p: No file to edit. Open ChatGPT → Apps → New App → paste URL below → Auth: No Auth → Create.

ol.li: Go to chatgpt.com → left sidebar → Apps.
Click New App → give it a name like Odoo.
Under Connection → select the Server URL tab.
Paste the URL above. Authentication → No Auth (key is in the URL).
Check "I understand and want to continue" → click Create.
Ask ChatGPT: "Show my overdue invoices"

div.div: ✅ This URL is ready to paste — your API key is already embedded. Works on Odoo.sh (public URL).

div.p: AI coding assistant by Anthropic with full MCP support. Works in your terminal.

ol.li: Install Claude Code: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
Run the command above once in your terminal.
Open Claude Code: claude
Test it: type "List my top 5 customers in Odoo"

div.p: AI-powered code editor with native MCP support. Connect Odoo data directly into your codebase workflow.

ol.li: Install Cursor from cursor.com (free plan available).
Create or open .cursor/mcp.json in your project root — or ~/.cursor/mcp.json for all projects.
Paste the JSON above. If mcpServers already exists, merge this entry inside it.
Save the file and restart Cursor.
Open Cursor Chat, type @ to activate Odoo tools.

div.p: Open-source AI coding assistant for VS Code and JetBrains with full MCP support.

ol.li: Install Continue extension in VS Code (Extensions → search "Continue") or JetBrains Marketplace.
Open ~/.continue/config.json (Continue sidebar → gear icon → Open config).
Add the JSON above inside the mcpServers array. Create the array if it doesn't exist.
Save. Reload VS Code: Ctrl+Shift+P → Developer: Reload Window.
In Continue chat, your Odoo tools are automatically available. Ask: "How many open invoices do I have?"

div.p: Uses mcp-remote as a stdio bridge. Requires Node.js. Two scenarios below — pick the one that matches your current config file.
div.div: ① File is empty or has only {} — paste this entire block:
② File already has other servers inside "mcpServers": { ... } — add only this entry inside the braces, with a comma after the previous entry:

ol.li: Confirm Node.js installed: run node -v in terminal. Missing? Download from nodejs.org.
Open Claude Desktop → Settings (top-right) → Developer → Edit Config.
Use Scenario ① if the file is empty. Use Scenario ② if it already has entries — add only the highlighted entry and put a comma after the entry above it.
Save the file. Fully quit and reopen Claude Desktop (Cmd+Q / Alt+F4, then relaunch).
Open a new chat — you'll see a hammer 🔨 icon. Click it to confirm Odoo tools appear.
Ask: "Show my top 5 customers in Odoo"

div.div: ✖ Common mistake: putting a comma after the last entry inside mcpServers. JSON does not allow trailing commas — this causes a parse error.

div.p: Google's open-source AI agent for the terminal. Free tier available with a Google account.

ol.li: Install Gemini CLI: npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
Authenticate: run gemini and follow the Google login prompt.
Open or create ~/.gemini/settings.json.
Paste the JSON above, merging into the mcpServers key.
Run gemini and ask: "What are my top customers by sales?"

div.p: OpenAI's terminal AI agent. Uses your OpenAI API key and supports MCP servers.

ol.li: Install Codex CLI: npm install -g @openai/codex
Set your OpenAI key: export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
Create or open ~/.codex/config.yaml and paste the YAML above.
Run codex in your terminal.
Ask: "Search Odoo for customers with overdue payments"

div.p: Open-source AI coding agent for the terminal. Supports multiple AI providers and MCP servers.

ol.li: Install OpenCode: npm install -g opencode-ai or visit opencode.ai.
Create opencode.json in your project root — or ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json globally.
Paste the JSON above and save.
Run opencode in your terminal.
Ask: "Show me my pending sale orders in Odoo"

div.p: Alternative: use a Python script to load Odoo tools into any OpenAI-compatible call. Works with localhost too.

div.pre:
# pip install openai mcp import asyncio from mcp import ClientSession from mcp.client.streamable_http import streamablehttp_client from openai import OpenAI
MCP_URL = "?key="
async def get_tools(): async with streamablehttp_client(MCP_URL) as (r, w, _): async with ClientSession(r, w) as s: await s.initialize() return (await s.list_tools()).tools
tools = asyncio.run(get_tools()) oai_tools = [{"type": "function", "function": { "name": t.name, "description": t.description, "parameters": t.inputSchema}} for t in tools]
client = OpenAI() # set OPENAI_API_KEY env var response = client.chat.completions.create( model="gpt-4o", tools=oai_tools, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Search for top 5 customers"}] )

ol.li: Install dependencies: pip install openai mcp
Set your OpenAI key: export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
Copy the script above into a file, e.g. odoo_ai.py.
Run it: python odoo_ai.py
Change the last line's content to your actual question. The script loads all Odoo MCP tools as OpenAI functions automatically.
